messages.message: Italy — this is Austria. Be frank: I am squeezed in the Balkans and cannot afford immediate hostility with you. I propose we avoid contesting Trieste/Venice this year. I will keep Trieste's fleet quiet (ALB/ADR moves only) if you do not press Trieste or Vienna, and if you coordinate any Adriatic moves with me. Specifically: A VIE hold, F TRI -> ALB, and you keep A VEN and F NAP where they are or use them against France if needed. If you agree, we can discuss a joint approach to the Adriatic/Balkans next year. If you do not agree, I must assume you will contest Trieste and prepare accordingly. Reply with plain yes/no and any modifications.

Turkey — Austria here. The Balkans are the immediate pressure point for both of us. I will not move into RUM or BUL this spring if you leave RUM/BUL uncontested for the moment and do not push north from CON/SMY into the Black Sea coast aggressively. My priority is holding BUD/VIE/TRI; your priority is keeping Turkey stable against Russia. If you keep F ANK and F CON posture defensive this turn, I will avoid moves into RUM and will support diplomatic coordination against Russian overreach. Are you willing to declare that posture and perhaps direct F ANK to hold? I need a clear commitment.

To all: England will maintain a cautious, naval posture this move. Our fleets in London and Edinburgh can cover the Channel and northern approaches comfortably; we prefer stable seas and a balance of power on the Continent. If any power contemplates a sudden northern push, note that our fleets can rapidly contest the North Sea and English Channel. I seek flexible understandings rather than rigid commitments—let us avoid a continental hegemon together.

Friend in Paris: I propose a simple understanding. I will not contest Brest or the western Channel if you avoid an early move against Belgium or the Low Countries that might push Germany north. In return I will move to secure the Channel lanes and keep an eye on the North Sea—F Lon and F Edi can support one another into NTH or hold the Channel. If Germany moves KIE→DEN or KIE→HEL, tell me and I will respond to protect Belgium/Holland from German naval-leaning expansion. We should coordinate so neither of us is surprised by a German naval thrust.

Berlin: England intends to keep sea lanes secure and avoid premature entanglement on the Continent. My fleets are positioned to contest the North Sea and Channel; any German advance toward the North Sea (KIE→DEN or KIE→HEL) will be met with British naval pressure and likely French unease. If your aim is an eastern continental sweep, signal that plainly and I will refrain from aggressive North Sea moves. Alternatively, propose a non-aggression understanding for the Low Countries so we both avoid an unnecessary naval confrontation.

Fellow powers — a frank word from Paris. I begin this year resolved to keep the peace in the west while securing France against any German overreach. My Mediterranean and Channel coasts are peaceful for now. I will move prudently: Marseilles and Paris can contest Iberia and Burgundy if needed, Brest will guard our sea-lanes. If Germany shows a northward thrust, I will respond where necessary. I prefer negotiated boundaries to wasted armies. Let us each announce intentions so accidental clashes do not happen.

England — salut. I value a cordial understanding between our fleets. I propose: you keep the North Sea and I respect the Channel. I plan a conservative Breton move this season (likely to Gascony or hold with Par/Mar support as needed) to avoid immediate friction. If you will avoid attacking Brest this year and keep LON/EDI focus north or at home, I will not contest the Channel fleet aggressively. This frees you to worry about the North Sea and Germany. Thoughts?

England — Germany here. Greetings. I intend to secure my northern flank this turn and avoid conflict with you. I will move my Kiel fleet to hold or to Denmark depending on your naval intentions. If you likewise avoid opening into the North Sea this move, we can coordinate: I will not contest English access to the Channel if you do not contest my access to Denmark/Holland. Please tell me your plans for the North Sea and whether you will support a peaceful status quo this spring. A frank exchange now will prevent dangerous two-front risk later.

France — Germany. I prefer clarity: I will not attack France this year if you remain neutral toward my eastern and northern moves. I plan to keep Munich and Berlin secure and will position one unit to deter Russian moves into Prussia. If you are willing to stay out of Ruhr/Burgundy/Belgium for now, I will not move on you. Can we agree to mutual restraint so we both avoid unnecessary early confrontation?

Russia — Germany. I am centrally placed and must guard against a two-front war. I will move to secure Prussia if you do not contest it. I propose we avoid immediate clashes: I will not push into Galicia or Prussia provided you refrain from moving aggressively into Prussia/Prussian coast. If you are planning an early westward thrust, tell me now; otherwise I will consider Prussia a neutral buffer this turn.

Friend in Vienna — greetings. We both sit close in the Adriatic; I prefer calm there this year. I will keep Venice and Rome peaceful if you leave Trieste alone (no moves into VEN/APU/ION). In return I will refrain from moving into TYR or supporting any power into your interior. If you agree, we can coordinate so neither of us wastes units contesting the Adriatic and focus elsewhere. Thoughts?

Sultan — Italy views the eastern Mediterranean with interest but prefers order to sudden war. I can move my fleet from Naples into the Ionian this season to keep an eye on the Aegean and protect Albania/Greek interests. If you wish, we can agree neutral zones: I will not contest Constantinople or Smyrna if you leave the southern Adriatic alone. Alternatively, I can help check any Austrian probing in the Adriatic. Would you like cooperation in the Ionian/Aegean this turn?

Paris — cordial note. I plan to keep a modest posture in the western Mediterranean; I can spare no hostility toward Provence this spring. If you promise not to push into Piedmont/Tyrol, I will not contest Marseilles or move into the Tyrrhenian to threaten you. A quiet western front lets us both watch central Europe; shall we agree to avoid an early clash?

Fellow powers — Russia here. The seas and steppes are open to diplomacy more than immediate bloodshed. I hold Moscow, St. Petersburg, Sevastopol and Warsaw and prefer orderly, mutually profitable arrangements. I will pursue a flexible northern approach while keeping an eye on the Black Sea. I propose: no surprise attacks on one another in the first two years while we discuss spheres — especially between England, Germany, and me in the north, and Austria, Turkey, and me in the south. Safe co-existence preserves options for everyone.

England — Russia. I value calm cooperation in the north. I can commit to avoiding aggressive moves toward Scandinavia (Norway/Sweden) if you respect my interest in Finland and the Baltic approaches this year. Proposed exchange: you focus on the British Isles and Channel; I will not contest Labrador/Norway openings and will support stability around St. Petersburg. If Germany eyes the Baltic, coordinate with me rather than strike alone.

Germany — Russia. Look to the long game: I prefer to avoid an early clash. I will not push on Prussia this spring if you avoid immediate moves through Prussia toward Warsaw or the Black Sea via Galicia. Let us delineate a temporary understanding: you keep your western fronts intact, I hold my western border; if Austria pressures you, warn me first and I will seek to mediate rather than escalate. Can we agree to share intel on movements near Prussia/Livonia?

Honored colleague in St. Petersburg — this is Constantinople. I write with calm firmness. My priority is secure Turkish borders and the safety of the Straits. I see our mutual interest in avoiding a Black Sea clash: a Russian fleet strong in Sevastopol and Turkish forces in the Black Sea will be a constant threat to both our coasts and commerce. I propose the following for the 1901 move season and beyond: 1) Non-aggression and deconfliction in the Black Sea — I will not move ANK/CON/SMY to actively contest Sevastopol this spring if you keep F SEV from aggressive moves toward the Bosporus/Armenia (no move to ARM or BLA). 2) A demilitarized zone approach for ARM and BLA — neither side builds or advances into ARM/BLA without prior agreement. 3) Intelligence and future talks — we exchange intentions for the next two seasons and coordinate if either of us needs to contest Rumelia/Bulgaria. These guarantees give me secure straits; they give you freedom in the Caucasus without fear of a Turkish rush. Please tell me your immediate moves this spring so we can avoid accidental clashes.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Italy agreed to keep Venice and Rome peaceful and not contest Trieste; Italy will avoid moves into TYR and will not support actions into Austrian interior. Austria offered to keep A VIE holding and F TRI to ALB (fleet to Albania or remain non-threatening in Adriatic). Turkey was asked to posture defensively (hold F ANK/F CON) and avoid pushing north into the Black Sea coast; Austria offered to refrain from moves into RUM/BUL if Turkey keeps defensive posture. Public messages from England, France, and Russia indicate they prefer cautious/stable early play; Russia explicitly proposes no surprise attacks in first two years and suggests negotiating southern spheres (relevant to Austria/Turkey). No explicit commitments received yet from Turkey in reply; Italy's reply was favorable and essentially a yes to the proposed non-aggression in the Adriatic.
Intent: S1901M orders I intend to give and will honor: A VIE - HOLD (I will hold Vienna as promised). F TRI -> ALB (move fleet Trieste to Albania to demonstrate non-threatening Adriatic posture). A BUD - HOLD (protect supply centers and avoid unnecessary provocation). I will fulfill the agreed moves with Italy. I intend to offer Turkey a reciprocal explicit request to HOLD F ANK and A CON for this turn; if Turkey refuses or moves north aggressively, I will pivot next turn to defend Galicia and secure Rumelia if possible. I will monitor Russia's Black Sea moves; if Russia shows pressure south (Sevastopol or Moldavia moves) I will prioritize supporting defensive coordination with Turkey and may seek German support. Key contingency: if Italy breaks the Adriatic understanding (moves into Trieste/Venice or supports into TYR), I will respond by contesting the Adriatic and seeking Turkish and German backing.

[S1901M] Trieste→Albania executed as promised; Vienna and Budapest held. Italy honored our Adriatic understanding by not contesting Trieste or Tyrol—good, trustworthy for now. Turkey also held Ankara and Constantinople as I requested by posture, though that was unrequested formally; both Turkey and Russia made no aggressive Black Sea moves. No betrayals this turn; I broke no promises.

Impact: I preserved 3 centers and showed restraint to secure alliances. Italy appears reliable; Turkey is presently cautious—both are usable partners to contain Russian southern pressure. Russia’s quiet posture is encouraging but watchful: four units remain a latent threat to Galicia and the Balkans.

Lessons: Continue clear, reciprocal asks (specific holds) and verify replies before committing further concessions. Maintain ready contingency to defend Galicia/Rumelia if Russia or Italy pivots. Keep fleet presence in Albania for flexibility and signaling.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I have emphasized a cautious naval posture to all. With France we agreed a tacit understanding: England will not contest Brest or aggressively attack French coasts this season if France avoids early moves into Belgium/the Low Countries that would push Germany north; France will respect the Channel and keep Brest conservative. With Germany I proposed mutual non-aggression in the Low Countries in exchange for Germany avoiding a North Sea/Denmark naval thrust; Germany signaled intent to secure its north but asked for my naval intentions. With Russia I agreed to focus on Isles/Channel and to respect Russian moves in Finland/the Baltic; Russia agreed to avoid aggressive Scandinavia moves this year. No formal binding alliances were made, only flexible understandings to avoid early naval escalation and a continental hegemon. I warned all powers that any Kiel→DEN/HEL or German northern naval move would prompt British naval pressure and likely French concern. Key commitments to remember: do not attack Brest; keep fleets focused on Channel/North Sea; respond assertively to German North Sea moves; respect Russia's Baltic/Finnish interest and France's western security.
Intent: S1901M orders intent: Hold a cautious

[S1901M] France kept to our tacit understanding — Brest moved only to Gascony and France pushed inland, not toward the Low Countries or Channel. Germany also kept its pledge for now: Kie→Den but no naval push into the North Sea or Denmark beyond that. No power attempted surprise naval incursions; all fleets respected sea lanes this turn. I honored my posture — fleets concentrated in Channel/North Sea (EDIN→NTH, LON→ENG) and troops pulled to CLY. No explicit betrayals; trustworthiness: France and Germany remain cautious and predictable; Russia, Italy, Austria, Turkey idle. Impact: status quo preserved — Britain retains maritime leverage and flexible diplomacy. Lesson: keep naval concentration visible to deter German northward escalation; continue subtle warnings about any Kie→DEN→HEL moves and cultivate French reassurance.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England and I agreed a cautious naval understanding: I will avoid contesting the North Sea and England will avoid attacking Brest this year and focus on LON/EDI north or home. England promised to respond if Germany navalizes from Kiel (KIE→DEN or KIE→HEL) to protect Belgium/Holland. Germany offered mutual restraint: no attack on France this year if I stay out of Ruhr/Burgundy/Belgium. Italy pledged no hostility toward Provence/Marseilles if I avoid Piedmont/Tyrol. Russia publicly proposed no surprise attacks in first two years but gave no bilateral commitment. No commitments from Austria or Turkey. Key diplomatic inferences to carry forward: Germany may open north (Kiel→Den/Hel) or east (Mun/Ber secure) — watch for KIE naval moves; England can be relied on to contest German naval thrusts if warned; Italy will not contest Marseilles this spring; staying out of Belgium/Low Countries preserves peace with Germany for now. Preserve Iberian influence as a bargaining chip next phase (Marseilles/Piedmont pressure vs Spain/Portugal possibilities).
Intent: S1901M orders (intentions I will issue): F BRE → GAS (Brest to Gascony) to show peaceful western posture while keeping coastal flexibility; A PAR → BUR (Paris to Burgundy) to cover/use Burgundy as a bargaining position but NOT to enter BEL/HOL; A MAR → PIE (Marseilles to Piedmont) only if Italy explicitly permits — otherwise A MAR supports hold (A MAR H) or moves to SPA/SC depending on Italy's confirmation. I intend to honor promises: I will not attack Belgium/Low Countries or directly challenge English Channel fleets this turn. I will monitor German KIE moves closely and immediately inform England if KIE→DEN or KIE

[S1901M] England kept to our naval understanding — she pulled LON→ENG and EDI→NTH, avoiding Brest; good, reliable partner against German naval thrusts. Germany honoured the limited restraint we inferred: KIE→DEN was played, not into Kiel-to-Hel/Belgium aggression, and both BER and MUN held. Italy respected the tacit non‑aggression toward Marseilles by allowing PIE move. I kept my commitments: Brest→Gas and Paris→Burgundy were peaceful, Marseilles→Piedmont occurred because Italy permitted. No one betrayed explicit promises this turn.

Surprises: Germany navalized from Kie to Den — exactly the opening I warned about; watch for a northward build or Baltic play. Impact: my western flank is secure for now; England is a dependable check on German sea moves. Lesson: preserve Iberian leverage (Burgundy/Piedmont pressure) for future bargaining, and immediately alert England if Kiel shifts again toward HEL or BEL.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] England: Agreed informal non-aggression at sea for this move if I refrain from entering the North Sea; England will keep a cautious naval posture and contest the North Sea/Channel if I advance north. France: Mutual restraint offered — I will not attack France this year if France stays neutral regarding Ruhr/Burgundy/Belgium. Russia: Reciprocal temporary understanding — Russia will not attack Prussia if I avoid moves through Prussia toward Warsaw or into Galicia; Russia offers to share intel on Prussian/Livonian moves and to mediate if Austria presses. No binding alliances formed; all understandings are tactical, limited to this phase to reduce two-front risk. Key commitments to record for future reference: do not move F KIE into the North Sea (DEN/HEL) this turn if relying on Anglo non-interference; do not attack France in 1901 if France holds neutrality; avoid moving into Warsaw/Galicia to honor Russia's assurance.
Intent: S1901M orders intent: Hold central security posture. Move F KIE to DEN (or KIE S) to secure northern flank without entering the North Sea proper; A BER hold to secure the North German border; A MUN hold to protect southern flank (Munich) and deter Austrian pressure. I intend to honor all negotiated non-aggression promises: I will not send F KIE into the North Sea (no KIE->HEL/ENH) and will not attack France this year. If England confirms Channel/NS restraint, I will execute KIE->DEN. If England signals aggression, revert F KIE to KIE hold. I will also refrain from moves into Warsaw/Galicia to respect Russia's request. I intend to fulfil these commitments.

[S1901M] I held to my commitments this turn: A BER and A MUN held, F KIE moved to DEN rather than risking the North Sea—England honored the informal restraint by moving NTH→ED1 and LON→ENG, so no naval interference. Russia and France remained neutral; Russia held Warsaw and Moscow, France vacated PAR→BUR but did not press into Germany. No betrayals occurred. Collaboration with England and Russia was tactically successful: northern flank secured without contest, reducing immediate two-front risk. Surprise: England’s fleets repositioned but did not threaten the Channel aggressively—encouraging but watchful. Impact: Germany maintains three SCs and central security. Lesson: continue recording specific, time-limited non-aggression pacts and require reciprocal visible moves (like England’s NTH/ENG behavior) before committing to risky naval advances.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] Austria: mutual non-aggression in the Adriatic — Austria will keep Trieste quiet (fleet limited to ALB/ADR) if Italy avoids contesting Trieste/Vienna and coordinates Adriatic moves; Austria asked for A VIE hold and F TRI -> ALB and to not press Trieste or Vienna. I replied I will keep Venice and Rome peaceful and will not move into VEN/APU/ION or support moves into Austria; open to coordinating next year. Turkey: I offered to move F NAP -> ION to watch the Aegean and protect Albania/Greek interests and proposed neutrality over Constantinople/Smyrna in exchange for Turkey leaving southern Adriatic alone / being checked against Austrian probing. France: I proposed mutual restraint in the west — I will not contest Marseilles or move into the Tyrrhenian if France refrains from pushing into Piedmont/Tyrol. England/Russia/Germany: public statements indicate caution/neutrals; no private deals yet. No binding commitments beyond the Adriatic-France understandings and the offered Ionian move to Turkey.
Intent: S1901M orders I intend to issue and will (barring betrayals) fulfill: 1) F NAP -> ION (as offered to Turkey) — intended to signal benign monitoring of eastern Med and to keep options open. 2) A VEN hold (or A VEN -> PIE if negotiations shift before orders locked) — current intent: hold to honor Austro non-aggression and avoid early clash. 3) A ROM -> TUS (or A ROM hold if I prefer safer build) — primary plan: A ROM -> TUS to pressure Tyrrhenian/central Italy and threaten MAR if France tests western coast; alternate: hold Rome if suspicious of French/German moves. I will fulfill F NAP

[S1901M] Austria honored our Adriatic understanding — Trieste fleet moved to ALB and Vienna held, so Austro remains reliable for now. I kept my word: Naples to Ionian and Rome to Tuscany executed, Venice held; no betrayals on our side. France, however, pressed into Piedmont (MAR -> PIE) despite our tacit west restraint—signals France may test Piedmont early; consider them opportunistic. No other surprises—Germany, England, Russia, Turkey passive. Strategically, controlling the Ionian legitimizes watching the eastern Med and placates Turkey; holding Venice preserved Austro trust. Lesson: verbal Adriatic pacts can work if reciprocated, but French intentions toward Piedmont require readiness to pivot — keep A VEN available to move into PIE next turn and maintain flexible diplomacy with Austria and Turkey to avoid being boxed in.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I opened for broadly non-aggressive, information-sharing agreements in both northern and southern theaters. With Germany I agreed to avoid immediate clashes: I will not push into Prussia this spring and expect Germany to refrain from moves through Prussia toward Warsaw or Galicia; we will share intel about Prussia/Livonia. With England I offered to avoid aggressive Scandinavian moves this year in exchange for English focus on the British Isles/Channel and respect for my interest in Finland/the Baltic approaches. With Turkey we reached a deconfliction understanding for the Black Sea: Turkey will not contest Sevastopol this spring if I keep F SEV from moves toward the Bosporus/Armenia (no move to ARM or BLA); we also discussed a tentative demilitarized-zone concept for ARM/BLA and exchanging intentions for the next two seasons. I signaled to Austria, France, and Italy a preference for orderly arrangements and no surprises but have no binding agreements with them yet. Public messages reiterate a two-year moratorium on surprise attacks and propose coordination with northern and southern partners. These agreements are conditional and aimed at preserving Russian flexibility while reducing risk of a two-front crisis this opening year.
Intent: Spring 1901 intent: preserve stability while keeping options open. Planned moves I intend to carry out consistent with negotiations: A WAR hold (defend Warsaw/limit provocation into Galicia), A MOS hold (conserve forces in depth), F STP/SC hold (protect St. Petersburg and Baltic approaches without entering Scandinavia), F SEV hold (no moves to ARM or BLA to honor Turkish deconfliction). I will not move into Prussia, Livonia, Norway, Sweden, Armenia, or the Black Sea this turn. [S1901M] Spring 1901: all parties honored the quiet opening I proposed. I held MOS, WAR, STP/SC and SEV as promised; Germany kept Prussia static, Turkey respected Sevastopol’s non-aggression, and England avoided Scandinavia. No betrayals detected. Austria and Italy made local, predictable moves; France pressed into Gascony and Piedmont but did not touch eastern fronts. The result: stability and time to build depth. Impact: my northern and southern cushions remain intact, giving tempo to choose fronts next year. Trustworthiness: Germany, Turkey, and England are cautiously reliable so far; others remain neutral. Lesson: public moratoriums plus explicit, narrow tactical commitments worked. Continue sharing intel, keep forces in reserve, and formalize short-term guarantees to lock in this calm.

full_private_diary:
[S1901M] I sought secure borders and calm over the Straits. Austria agreed to avoid moves into RUM/BUL this spring if I keep F ANK and F CON defensive; they asked me to publicly hold F ANK. Italy offered to patrol the Ionian and avoid contesting CON/SMY if I keep the southern Adriatic alone; potential cooperative patrol of the Aegean/Ionian was proposed. Russia and I agreed a conditional non‑aggression: I will not contest Sevastopol this spring if F SEV does not move toward the Bosporus or Armenia (no move to BLA or ARM). We discussed a DMZ approach for ARM and BLA and exchanging intentions for the next two seasons to avoid accidental clashes. England and France signaled general western caution but no direct commitments with me. I have explicit commitments from Austria and a verbal agreement with Russia; Italy offered cooperative neutrality in the eastern Mediterranean contingent on my posture. Key risk: Russia may still seek Balkan influence via RUM/BUL if commitments break; Austria may change if threatened. Keep the Straits and Black Sea safety central.
Intent: S1901M orders intent: Hold F ANK and F CON to demonstrate defensive posture and honor the commitment to Austria and Italy; A SMY -> A ANK? No — keep A SMY hold as well to avoid provocative moves toward the Balkans. Specifically: F ANK H, F CON H, A SMY H (no moves into BUL/CON/ARM/BLA). I intend to fulfil these promises to secure guarantees and maintain a de‑escalatory stance in the Black Sea and Balkans. I will inform Russia of these exact holds and request confirmation of F SEV holding (no move to BLA/ARM). I will coordinate with Italy on an agreed Ionian/Ionian-Aegean

[S1901M] I honored my commitments: F ANK, F CON and A SMY held as promised to Austria, Italy and Russia. Austria likewise held VIE and BUD and moved TRI→ALB as agreed, showing cooperative intent in the south. Russia kept F SEV and its armies at home; no moves toward the Bosporus or Armenia—our conditional non‑aggression held this turn. Italy moved to ION as discussed, supporting eastern Mediterranean stability. No betrayals occurred.

Impact: my borders and control of the Straits remain secure; the Black Sea is calm for now, buying time to build trust and seek formal DMZ guarantees. Trustworthiness: Austria and Russia reliable this phase; Italy cooperative. Lesson: continue clear, public promises plus reciprocal visible holds to lock in behavior. Next: press Russia for a written DMZ on ARM/BLA and explore joint patrol wording with Italy to deter surprises.
